description:
compile_fn opens with ~230 lines of entry-harness setup (lines ~3667-3900) that:
  1. Creates the Cranelift entry block
  2. Branches on (is_native, is_cont_fn, closure_target_n_caps) to set up params
  3. Populates var_env (after T5) with entry-block param bindings
  4. Returns (frame_ptr, host_ctx, cont_param)

Extract this into:
  fn build_entry_harness<M: cranelift_module::Module>(
      b: &mut FunctionBuilder<'_>,
      jmod: &mut M,
      env: &CodegenEnv<'_>,        // after T6
      f: &fz_ir::FnIr,
      this_spec_id: u32,
      is_native: bool,
      is_cont_fn: bool,
      closure_target_n_caps: Option<usize>,
      entry_cl: ir::Block,
      var_env: &mut HashMap<u32, VarBinding>,  // after T5
  ) -> (Option<ir::Value>, Option<ir::Value>, Option<ir::Value>)
       // (frame_ptr, host_ctx, cont_param)

The body is the existing 3-way branch. compile_fn calls it immediately after sealing entry_cl
and uses the returned tuple for the rest of compilation.

After T1: fns_needing_host_ctx is gone, so host_ctx is always None for native fns.
The cont-fn branch at line 3792 already returns None unconditionally after T1.
After T5: var_env replaces var_map+var_reprs.
After T6: env replaces the bundled params.

cargo build must produce zero warnings. All tests must pass.
title: fz-cg2.7 extract build_entry_harness from compile_fn
